calloff_simulator.long: Call-Off Simulator shows where the week is already thin and which employees are the best manager backup options. Pair it with the Replacement / Swap Assistant for advisory backup options that still require manager approval.
calloff_simulator.short: Review backup staffing options before someone calls out.

generate_fresh.long:
Use Generate Fresh when you want the solver to create a new baseline schedule for the selected week label.

Best practice:
1) Confirm staffing requirements and store hours first.
2) Generate Fresh.
3) Review warnings and heatmap.
4) Use Refine to improve weak spots before publishing.
generate_fresh.short: Build a brand-new weekly schedule from current rules.

generate_regen.long:
Regenerate From Current creates a new schedule while preserving continuity context from the current schedule.

Use this when requirements changed and you want an updated schedule without manually rebuilding everything.
generate_regen.short: Regenerate while preserving context from current schedule.

load_final.long: Load Final restores the saved final schedule for the current week so a manager can review, compare, or re-export the approved version.
load_final.short: Reload the already-published final for this week.

manager_report_export.long:
This report summarizes publish readiness, shortage windows, and call-list suggestions in plain manager language.

Use it after Generate or Refine and before locking the final schedule.
manager_report_export.short: Open the manager review report with publish-readiness details.

open_heatmap.long:
The heatmap compares scheduled staffing to requirement targets by day/time/area.

Red indicates under-minimum coverage.
Use this first to find where labor is missing.
open_heatmap.short: Open the coverage heatmap for demand vs scheduled staffing.

open_manual.long:
Manual Edit lets managers adjust cells directly.

Recommended sequence:
- Load current schedule
- Make edits
- Analyze warnings
- Apply changes
- Refine if needed
open_manual.short: Open manual editing tools in a popup.

print_html.long: Open Print View exports the main HTML schedule and then tries to open it locally using the safest available method for your operating system.
print_html.short: Open the printable schedule in your default browser/app.

publish_final.long: Publish Final saves the current week as the final manager-approved version used for review, reprint, and next-week continuity. Review the Publish Readiness Checklist first whenever possible.
publish_final.short: Lock this week as the published final schedule.

refine_schedule.long:
Refine keeps the current schedule as baseline and applies targeted improvements.

By default manual edits are protected; enable the toggle only if you want refine to adjust unlocked manual entries.
refine_schedule.short: Refine current schedule to improve weak spots.

reprint_final.long: Reprint Final recreates printable outputs from the saved final without asking the solver to regenerate the week.
reprint_final.short: Rebuild exports from the published final schedule.

req_apply.long:
Writes Min/Preferred/Max requirement blocks for the selected area, days, and time range.

Use this for quick week setup and repeatable templates.
req_apply.short: Apply staffing pattern to selected days.

req_copy.long:
Useful when weekday demand patterns are similar.

Copy, then make small per-day adjustments for peaks and events.
req_copy.short: Copy one day's requirement pattern to other days.

save_history.long: Saves this version for later comparison and rollback support. Historical Suggestions reuses learned history as advisory guidance only and does not change requirements automatically.
save_history.short: Store current week result in schedule history.

store_hours.long:
Hours of operation are hard scheduling boundaries and control visible heatmap hours.

Set these before applying requirement patterns.

store_state.long:
Only the selected Store-state profile is applied at runtime.

If a profile is incomplete, the app safely falls back to conservative defaults.
store_state.short: Select the store state used for labor-law enforcement.
